source_paper_title: Erratum to: Linear Convergence of Dual Coordinate Descent on Nonpolyhedral Convex Problems
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Necoara_2025, title={Erratum to: Linear Convergence of Dual Coordinate Descent on Nonpolyhedral Convex Problems}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2024.0500}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2024.0500}, number={3}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Necoara, Ion and Fercoq, Olivier}, year={2025}, month=aug, pages={1935–1938} }
context:
Page 13, end of Section 3.2 (immediately after Theorem 3 and its discussion).

Our main result of this section states that we have on any sublevel set a quadratic growth condition on the dual of (3), provided that \(g\) is smooth strongly convex function and the sets \(X_i\) satisfy Slater’s condition. We conjecture that a similar result holds by replacing the Slater’s condition with the linear regularity condition (5). We will investigate this conjecture in our future work.
title: Quadratic growth of the dual objective under linear regularity of constraints
background_latex:
Consider the constrained convex optimization problem
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^n} g(x) \quad \text{s.t.}\quad x\in X:=\bigcap_{i=1}^m X_i,
\]
where:
1) \(g:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\) is differentiable, \(1\)-strongly convex, and has \(L\)-Lipschitz continuous gradient.
2) Each \(X_i\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\) is a closed convex set, and projections onto individual \(X_i\) are assumed computationally easy.
3) The feasible set is \(X=\cap_{i=1}^m X_i\), assumed nonempty.

Define the (global) linear regularity property for the collection \(\{X_i\}_{i=1}^m\): there exists \(\mu\in(0,1]\) such that
\[
\mu\,\mathrm{dist}^2(x,X) \le \frac{1}{m}\sum_{i=1}^m \mathrm{dist}^2(x,X_i)\qquad \forall x\in\mathbb{R}^n,
\]
where \(\mathrm{dist}(x,S)=\inf_{z\in S}\|x-z\|\).

Form the standard dual by introducing replicated variables \((x,x_1,\dots,x_m)\) with coupling constraints \(x=x_i\) for all \(i\), leading to a dual variable \(y=(y_1;\dots;y_m)\in\mathbb{R}^{mn}\). The dual objective (to be minimized) has the composite form
\[
 d(y) := g^*\!\left(-\sum_{i=1}^m y_i\right) + \sum_{i=1}^m \mathrm{supp}_{X_i}(y_i),
\]
where \(g^*\) is the Fenchel conjugate of \(g\), and \(\mathrm{supp}_{X_i}(y)=\sup_{x\in X_i}\langle y,x\rangle\) is the support function.

Let \(d^*:=\inf_{y\in\mathbb{R}^{mn}} d(y)\) and \(Y^*:=\arg\min_{y\in\mathbb{R}^{mn}} d(y)\) denote the dual optimal value and dual solution set.

A (local) quadratic growth property of \(d\) on a given sublevel set \(\{y: d(y)\le d(y_0)\}\) means: there exists \(\sigma>0\) (possibly depending on \(y_0\)) such that
\[
 d(y)-d^* \ge \frac{\sigma}{2}\,\mathrm{dist}^2(y,Y^*) \qquad \forall y\in\mathbb{R}^{mn}\ \text{with}\ d(y)\le d(y_0).
\]
The paper proves such a quadratic growth statement under Slater-type constraint qualification (a relative-interior point condition allowing some sets to be polyhedral). It also proves strong duality under linear regularity, but does not establish quadratic growth under linear regularity.
problem_definition_latex:
Assume that \(g\) is differentiable, \(1\)-strongly convex, and has \(L\)-Lipschitz gradient, and that the closed convex sets \(\{X_i\}_{i=1}^m\) have nonempty intersection \(X\) and satisfy the global linear regularity property
\[
\mu\,\mathrm{dist}^2(x,X) \le \frac{1}{m}\sum_{i=1}^m \mathrm{dist}^2(x,X_i)\qquad \forall x\in\mathbb{R}^n
\]
for some \(\mu\in(0,1]\).

For the dual objective
\[
 d(y) := g^*\!\left(-\sum_{i=1}^m y_i\right) + \sum_{i=1}^m \mathrm{supp}_{X_i}(y_i),
\]
determine whether the following property holds:

For every \(y_0\in\mathbb{R}^{mn}\), there exists \(\sigma=\sigma(y_0)>0\) such that
\[
 d(y)-d^* \ge \frac{\sigma}{2}\,\mathrm{dist}^2(y,Y^*) \qquad \forall y\in\mathbb{R}^{mn}\ \text{with}\ d(y)\le d(y_0),
\]
where \(d^*=\inf_y d(y)\) and \(Y^*=\arg\min_y d(y)\).

location_in_paper: Page 13, end of Section 3.2 (immediately after Theorem 3 and its discussion).
open_problem_quote: Our main result of this section states that we have on any sublevel set a quadratic growth condition on the dual of (3), provided that \(g\) is smooth strongly convex function and the sets \(X_i\) satisfy Slater’s condition. We conjecture that a similar result holds by replacing the Slater’s condition with the linear regularity condition (5). We will investigate this conjecture in our future work.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can algorithmically generate families of convex sets (e.g., via epigraph/hypograph descriptions or intersections of balls/second-order cones) that satisfy linear regularity but violate Slater, and then numerically probe the dual objective for failure of a quadratic growth inequality on chosen sublevel sets. However, certifying linear regularity and certifying violation of quadratic growth globally are both nontrivial, so automated search would likely rely on heuristics and finite-dimensional discretizations.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate counterexamples can be parameterized (e.g., by defining sets \(X_i\) through simple projections), and a fitness signal can be based on numerically estimating the smallest local quadratic growth constant over sampled points in a sublevel set while checking approximate linear regularity. The signal is computationally accessible but imperfect because both properties involve worst-case quantifiers that are hard to certify exactly.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The open problem asks whether global linear regularity of the constraint sets \(\{X_i\}\) suffices to guarantee a sublevel-set quadratic growth (error bound) for the composite dual objective \(d(y)=g^*(-\sum_i y_i)+\sum_i \mathrm{supp}_{X_i}(y_i)\). The originating work by Necoara and Fercoq proves this quadratic growth under a Slater-type relative-interior condition (allowing some polyhedral sets) and explicitly conjectures that linear regularity might be enough, but does not establish it.

With no forward-citation evidence provided here, there is currently no identified paper that resolves the conjecture. Existing background results connect Slater-type conditions to bounded linear regularity (Bauschke--Borwein--Li) and show quadratic growth for duals in polyhedral/linear-constraint settings (e.g., Necoara--Nedelcu) or for augmented formulations under compactness (Gidel--Pedregosa--Lacoste-Julien). However, none of these directly implies quadratic growth of \(d\) under mere global linear regularity for general (possibly nonpolyhedral, unbounded) sets.

Promising directions suggested by the source paper’s proof strategy include: (i) deriving a decomposition of the dual solution set \(Y^*\) into a subspace plus a compact set (as done under Slater) using only linear regularity; and/or (ii) proving an error bound for the KKT mapping \(0\in \partial d(y)\) from metric regularity/subtransversality properties implied by linear regularity. Establishing such a link would likely require additional assumptions (e.g., amenability, definability, or some form of constraint qualification beyond linear regularity) or a counterexample demonstrating failure in full generality.

sources.title: Linear convergence of dual coordinate descent on non-polyhedral convex problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Proves local quadratic growth of the dual objective \(d(y)=g^*(-\sum_i y_i)+\sum_i \mathrm{supp}_{X_i}(y_i)\) on any sublevel set under a Slater-type condition (allowing some \(X_i\) polyhedral), yielding linear convergence of randomized (accelerated) dual coordinate descent and randomized Dykstra.
sources.connection: This is the originating work that poses the open question: whether the same sublevel-set quadratic growth of \(d\) holds assuming only global linear regularity of \(\{X_i\}\), without Slater/relative-interior assumptions.

sources.title: Strong conical hull intersection property, bounded linear regularity, Jameson property, and error bounds in convex optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops relationships among geometric regularity properties of convex set collections (including bounded linear regularity) and error bounds; provides conditions under which Slater-type assumptions imply bounded linear regularity.
sources.connection: Provides foundational results connecting Slater/relative-interior conditions to (bounded) linear regularity; relevant for assessing whether linear regularity alone could imply the dual quadratic growth/error bound sought in the open problem.

sources.title: Linear convergence of first order methods for non-strongly convex optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes linear convergence of first-order methods under error bound/quadratic growth conditions and discusses equivalences and implications among these regularity notions.
sources.connection: Supplies the algorithmic motivation and the equivalence between quadratic growth and error bounds used in the original paper; helps frame what must be proved for \(d\) under linear regularity.

sources.title: On linear convergence of a distributed dual gradient algorithm for linearly constrained separable convex problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Proves dual error bounds/linear convergence for dual gradient methods in linearly constrained settings, leveraging strong convexity of the primal objective.
sources.connection: Represents the polyhedral/linear-constraint analogue where dual quadratic growth is known; serves as a comparison point for extending to general convex-set intersections under linear regularity.

sources.title: Frank-Wolfe splitting via augmented Lagrangian method
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows quadratic growth for certain augmented dual/ALM-based formulations under compactness assumptions and Slater-type conditions, enabling faster rates for splitting methods.
sources.connection: Cited in the source paper as related work proving quadratic growth under boundedness/compactness; highlights that removing such assumptions (or replacing Slater by linear regularity) is nontrivial.
